# feat(kaizen-agents): SPEC-03 composition wrappers with stack ordering

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `5a9635e74ef8` — feat(kaizen-agents): SPEC-03 composition wrappers with stack ordering

**Body**:

New kaizen_agents composition wrappers for BaseAgent:
- WrapperBase: abstract base with _inner proxy, duplicate detection,
  and canonical stack ordering enforcement via _WRAPPER_PRIORITY map
- GovernedAgent: trust/envelope governance wrapper
- MonitoredAgent: cost/budget tracking wrapper with BudgetExhaustedError
- StreamingAgent: outermost wrapper, emits typed StreamEvent stream
- events module: TextDelta, ToolCallStart/End, TurnComplete,
  BudgetExhausted, ErrorEvent, StreamBufferOverflow, StreamTimeoutError

Bug fixes from the deep red team:
- StreamingAgent.run_stream() now emits ToolCallStart/ToolCallEnd
  events per tool call in the TAOD result, and reads real iteration
  count from result metadata (was hardcoded to 1)
- WrapperBase raises WrapperOrderError if a wrapper is applied on top
  of a higher-priority wrapper (enforces the canonical stacking
  BaseAgent -> Governed -> Monitored -> Streaming)
- Delegate facade refactored to compose wrapper stack internally
  (SPEC-05): AgentLoop -> [L3GovernedAgent] -> [MonitoredAgent]
- pyproject.toml: declare kaizen/kaizen_agents/kailash as first-party
  in both isort and ruff to stop import-sorter ping-pong

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
